figures.caption: Figure 2: Emotion-eliciting decision shift for different model - game combination. We can observe a obvious difference between positive emotions and negative emotions. Annotations indicate FDR-adjusted significance thresholds (* (q<0.05), ** (q<0.01), *** (q<0.001))

Figure 1: To better understand the impact of emotion on agents’ decision-making. Our study builds a decision dataset covering multi-modal, multi-agent, multi-turn cases. And steering model representation to emotion directions.

Table 3: Overall results on normalized drift magnitude and normalized aligned drift with a comparision between raw output and CoT mode. ‘Mean_Random’ column shows the mean results under a random steering vector. ‘Mean_Emotions’ column shows mean results over all emotion steerings.
Table 1: Data distribution across games and source. (Legend: ⟳ Multi-Turn; ♢Multi-Modal; •Multi-Agent)
Table 7: Directional influence of emotions on game behavior: ↑ (increases behavior), ↓ (decreases behavior), ◦ (neutral/weak effect).
